questions.q: Which African country is home to the majority of the world's Egyptian pyramids?
What is the capital of South Africa?
The Sahara Desert covers most of which African region?
Which African country is named after a major river that flows through it?
Victoria Falls is located on the border between which two African nations?
What is the largest country in Africa by land area?
Which African capital city is famous for its Table Mountain landmark?
The Great Rift Valley runs through which parts of Africa?
Which African country was formerly known as Rhodesia?
What is the most widely spoken language across Africa?
Which island nation lies off the southeast coast of Africa?
The ancient kingdom of Nubia was located in which modern-day African region?
Which West African country is known as the 'Gateway to Africa' and has Lagos as its largest city?
The Congo River is the second-longest river in Africa. Which country does it flow through primarily?
Which African country is completely surrounded by South Africa?
What is the capital of Kenya?
The Serengeti National Park, famous for its wildlife migration, is located in which African country?
Which African country has the most official languages, with 11 recognized at the national level?
The ancient kingdom of Axum was centered in which modern African country?
Which African country is known for its iconic Victoria Falls?
What is the largest desert in Africa?
Which country is home to the Great Rift Valley?
What is the most populous country in Africa?
In what year did South Africa end apartheid and hold its first democratic election?
Which language is most widely spoken across Africa?
Which country won the African Cup of Nations most frequently?
What is the capital of Nigeria?
Which African country is home to the ancient ruins of Great Zimbabwe?
What is the capital of Morocco?
Which country shares the name with an ancient North African empire that rivaled Rome?
What is the capital of Ethiopia?
Which African nation is known for its traditional Maasai warrior culture?
What is the capital city of Senegal, which also hosts the finish line of the famous Paris-Dakar rally?
Which African island nation inspired the animated film about a lion, zebra, giraffe, and hippo?
Which major waterfall forms the border between Zambia and Zimbabwe?
Which African country's name means 'free' in English?
Which West African nation is primarily French-speaking and known for its rich music and cultural heritage?
Which African nation is known as 'Africa in Miniature' due to its diverse geography and wildlife?
Which African city is located at approximately the same latitude as Dallas, Texas?
The Zambezi River flows through which of these African countries?
Which African nation was previously called Nyasaland before gaining independence?
What is the southernmost country on the African continent?
The 1974 coup d'état that overthrew Emperor Haile Selassie in Ethiopia was led by which military committee, known by a name meaning 'the committee' in Amharic?
Which African nation's civil war (1983-2005) resulted in one of the highest death tolls of any conflict in the post-Cold War era?
Lake Tanganyika, the world's deepest freshwater lake, borders which four African countries?
Which West African empire, lasting from roughly the 13th to 16th centuries, was famous for Timbuktu's scholarly institutions and Mansa Musa's pilgrimage?
The Great Zimbabwe civilization, which flourished from the 11th to 15th centuries, traded extensively with merchants from which distant region?
Mount Stanley, Africa's third-highest peak, straddles the border between Uganda and which other nation?
The Bantu migration, one of history's largest human population movements, began roughly when and from which region of Africa?
Which mineral resource, abundantly found in Angola, Botswana, and South Africa, was central to the 'conflict diamonds' controversy of the 1990s and 2000s?
The Malagasy language, spoken in Madagascar, belongs to which language family despite the island's African location?
In 1960, which African country became the first to gain independence south of the Sahara?
The Rwandan genocide of 1994 resulted in approximately how many deaths in just 100 days?
Which African nation served as a sanctuary for Liberian and Sierra Leonean refugees and played a key role in regional peacekeeping despite its own civil war?
The Sahel region experiences desertification partly due to the expansion of which desert?
Which Southern African nation was the last on the continent to gain independence from colonial rule, doing so in 1990?
The Rosetta Stone, instrumental in deciphering Egyptian hieroglyphics, was discovered during which military campaign in 1799?
choices: The Ottoman expansion into North Africa

questions.q: Which African country has the longest coastline on the continent, stretching over 7,000 kilometers?
The Nok civilization, known for distinctive terracotta figurines, flourished in which modern African nation between roughly 1500 BCE and 500 CE?
Which deadly disease, responsible for millions of deaths in Central Africa, is transmitted by the tsetse fly?
The Scramble for Africa culminated in the Berlin Conference of 1884-1885 where European powers divided the continent. Which African nation successfully resisted colonization during this period?
Which African country was formerly known as Upper Volta until its name change in 1984?
Which African nation is home to the source of the Blue Nile, located in the highlands near Lake Tana?
The Central African Republic shares its eastern border with which large East African nation?
Which West African country has French as its official language but is named after a major river that forms part of its border?
Mount Speke and Mount Baker are major peaks located on which African mountain range known for its glaciers?
The Aswan High Dam, completed in 1970, created Lake Nasser and is located in which African country?
Which landlocked African nation was ruled by Idi Amin from 1971 to 1979?
Which Saharan nation was the first to sign the Treaty of Ouagadougou, establishing what is now the African Union?
The Seychelles archipelago consists of approximately how many islands scattered across the Indian Ocean?
Which African capital city sits at the confluence of the White Nile and Blue Nile rivers?
The Seren Rondelle, also known as the Benin Bronzes, were historical artifacts looted from which West African kingdom?
Which African country experienced a genocide in 1994 where approximately 800,000 people were killed in approximately 100 days?
The Drakensberg Mountains form a natural border between South Africa and which neighboring country?
Which African nation adopted the Euro as its currency in 2002, despite being located on the continent?
The Banda Islands, historically a center of spice production, are located in which African nation's Indian Ocean territory?
Which North African city served as the capital of the Ottoman Empire's Regency from the 16th to 19th centuries?
The Great Zimbabwe ruins, dating to around the 11th century, were built by which African civilization?
Which African country is home to the Skeleton Coast, a treacherous stretch of Atlantic shoreline littered with shipwrecks?
Which West African nation was established in 1822 as a settlement for freed American slaves?
Lake Tanganyika, Africa's deepest freshwater lake, is shared by Tanzania, Zambia, the Democratic Republic of Congo, and which fourth country?
Which North African country gained independence from Italy in 1951?
Which African country is home to the ancient rock-hewn churches of Lalibela, carved from monolithic stone in the 12th century?
Which Moroccan city served as a major center of Islamic learning and was home to the University of Al Quaraouiyine, founded in 859 CE?
Which Central African nation has Bangui as its capital and was formerly known as the Central African Empire under Emperor Bokassa?
The ancient kingdom of Axum, a major trading power in the first centuries CE, was located in what is now which African nation?
Mount Kenya, Africa's second-highest peak, stands in which East African nation?
The Danakil Depression, one of the lowest and hottest places on Earth, is located in which African country?
Lake Tanganyika, one of Africa's largest freshwater lakes, is bordered by four countries. Which of these is NOT one of them?
In what year did Eritrea gain independence from Ethiopia, becoming Africa's newest nation at that time?
The Great Rift Valley runs through several African countries. Which East African nation contains the largest portion of this geological formation?
Mount Kilimanjaro, Africa's highest peak, is located in which country?
Which African country has the second-longest river on the continent after the Nile?
Which Southern African country became fully independent in 1966, taking its name from a local people?
Which African nation is home to the headquarters of the African Union, established in its capital city?
The island nation of Mauritius gained independence from British rule in which year?
Which North African country is bordered by Libya to the east and Morocco to the west?
The Okavango Delta, a unique inland river delta ecosystem, is primarily located in which country?
Which African country was divided into North and South until their reunification in 1990?
Which West African nation is the world's largest producer of cocoa beans?
The Cape of Good Hope, a major maritime landmark, is located at the southern tip of which African country?
Which East African country is known for being landlocked and named after a lake that forms part of its border?
The Serengeti ecosystem spans the border between Tanzania and which other East African nation?
The Kruger National Park, one of Africa's largest game reserves, is located in which country?
Which Southern African country adopted a new constitution in 1994 that formally ended apartheid?
Lake Victoria, the world's second-largest freshwater lake, is bordered by Tanzania, Uganda, and which third country?
The ancient kingdom of Kush, which rivaled Egypt's power, was centered in which modern-day country?
Which East African nation has the most linguistically diverse population, with over 120 indigenous languages spoken?
The Nubian Pyramids, distinct from those in Egypt, are concentrated in which modern African country?
Which landlocked Southern African country shares borders with eight other nations?
Which West African nation experienced a devastating civil war from 1989 to 2003 and has since become a beacon of post-conflict reconstruction?
The Kalahari Desert is primarily located in which Southern African country?
Which North African country is home to the Sahara's largest sand sea, known as the Grand Erg?
The Dogon people, known for their astronomical knowledge and distinctive architecture, primarily inhabit which West African country?
Lake Tanganyika, one of Africa's largest lakes, borders which four countries?
choices: Tanzania, Democratic Republic of Congo, Zambia, Burundi

questions.q: Which African nation is home to the ancient city of Timbuktu, a historical center of Islamic learning?
Which North African country has Algiers as its capital?
The Victoria Falls, one of the world's largest waterfalls, straddles the border between Zambia and which other country?
The Pan-African Highway network connects African countries by road. Which organization established this initiative?
Which African country's name derives from the Swahili word meaning 'to shine'?
The Horn of Africa region includes Ethiopia, Somalia, and which other country?
